title: Persist full content items in offline cache
description: Extend CachedEntryData in Provider.swift to persist the full WidgetContentItem (Thread or Article) so that makeOfflineTimeline() can reconstruct WidgetEntry with contentItem != nil, enabling the 'last-good cached entries rendered normally' behavior in the offline state.
acceptance_criteria: CachedEntryData encodes/decodes Thread and Article content items
makeOfflineTimeline() produces WidgetEntry with non-nil contentItem when cache exists
Offline widget entries with cached content render title, source, and date (not 'Can't update')

handoff_summary.completed: - Thread and Article model structs upgraded from Decodable to Codable by adding matching encode(to:) implementations using existing CodingKeys. - WidgetContentItem enum given full Codable conformance via a discriminated-union approach (type key + payload key). - CachedEntryData gained a contentItem: WidgetContentItem? field. - buildTimeline() now populates contentItem in every CachedEntryData it saves. - makeOfflineTimeline() now passes data.contentItem into WidgetEntry, so offline entries have a non-nil contentItem whenever cache exists. Build verified clean.
handoff_summary.risks: Existing on-device cache files written by previous app versions will decode contentItem as nil (the field is optional, so old JSON silently skips it). The first online refresh after the update will write a fully-populated cache; until then, offline entries fall back to the same nil-contentItem behavior as before — no regression.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify the three acceptance criteria: cache round-trip encoding/decoding, non-nil contentItem from makeOfflineTimeline, and widget view rendering of title/source/date from cached entries.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Thread.swift and Article.swift are shared model files used by both the app and widget targets. Any sibling bead that also touches these files will need to merge carefully. No other bead in this feature tree is expected to touch models.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Thread and Article needed Encodable to enable full-content caching. Adding encode(to:) manually (mirroring the existing init(from:) + CodingKeys) was chosen over alternatives like storing raw JSON Data in CachedEntryData, because it keeps the cache format typed and self-describing, and avoids double-serialization complexity. WidgetContentItem uses a two-key discriminated union { "type": "thread"|"article", "thread"|"article": {...} } rather than a flat keyed envelope, which is unambiguous and future-proof if a third case is ever added. The contentItem field in CachedEntryData is optional so that caches written by older app versions decode without error — they just get nil, which preserves the existing offline fallback behavior.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) CachedEntryData round-trips correctly via JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder for both .thread and .article cases — especially that the discriminator key correctly selects the right case on decode. (2) After buildTimeline runs with live data, a subsequent call to makeOfflineTimeline returns WidgetEntries whose contentItem is non-nil and matches the cached item. (3) Old cache JSON (no contentItem field) decodes without crashing and produces contentItem == nil entries, falling back to .offline state. (4) Widget views render the thread's representativeTitle / article's title, sourceName, and date fields when contentItem is non-nil in an offline entry.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Cache files from app versions prior to this change will not contain contentItem; the first successful online fetch will write the updated format. Until then, offline entries still show nil contentItem (existing fallback, no regression). The widgetState remains .offline even when contentItem is non-nil — widget views are responsible for deciding whether to show a stale-data indicator alongside the content; this bead only supplies the data, not the view logic.

conflict_risks: Thread.swift and Article.swift are shared model files used by both the app and widget targets. Any sibling bead that also touches these files will need to merge carefully. No other bead in this feature tree is expected to touch models.

execution_history.summary: Extended CachedEntryData to persist full WidgetContentItem (Thread or Article) and updated makeOfflineTimeline() to reconstruct WidgetEntry with non-nil contentItem from the cache. This enables offline widget entries to render title, source, and date rather than a generic 'Can't update' state.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
